title: Coelopleurum saxatile
intro: Coelopleurum saxatile, also known as Saxatile or Yarrow-like Coelopleurum, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of East Asia, characterized by its clusters of small white flowers and divided leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which aid in reducing pain and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Coelopleurum saxatile include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Coelopleurum saxatile is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is also used to make teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Coelopleurum saxatile is classified as a genus of flowering plants in the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is native to East Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and the Russian Far East. The genus Coelopleurum contains two species of plants, including Coelopleurum saxatile. Coelopleurum saxatile is a perennial herb that grows in mountainous regions. This plant is often found in rocky or sandy areas, and its classification helps us understand its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
